entries.decision: vision.md + scope.md -> preserve as historical design records with a status banner; constraints.md -> split (constraints current, decision-log historical); questions.md -> update assumptions to validated/partial/open with evidence. Banner: '> Status: historical design record (2026-06-09). For current state see the README.'
entries.rationale: Pre-build design intent is worth preserving but must not read as current claims; a one-line banner + a link to current state is the cheapest honest fix.

entries.context: F2 which README is the adversary
entries.decision: The repo-root README is the adversarial, humble, evidence-ruled front door (and vault root); plugin/README stays install-focused and links to the root for the why/limitations.
entries.rationale: Root README is the GitHub face and the vault root note a reader meets first; the in-plugin README serves the already-installing reader.

entries.decision: Relative markdown links across all docs (not [[wikilinks]]).
entries.rationale: Relative markdown links render on BOTH GitHub - where adopters first meet the repo - and Obsidian; wikilinks render oddly on GitHub.

entries.decision: Canonical install instructions live in the root README; plugin/README links to them rather than duplicating.
entries.rationale: Single source of truth avoids drift; root README is where a deciding user lands.

entries.decision: Add an MIT LICENSE file at root to match plugin.json's declared license.
entries.rationale: The manifest already asserts MIT; the unbacked claim is fixed by adding the file, not weakening the manifest.

entries.decision: Do not commit an .obsidian/ folder; rely on the convention that any folder is a vault.
entries.rationale: Committed Obsidian config churns per-machine and adds no value; relative links make the graph navigable without it.

entries.decision: Inline [E] (evidenced, with citation) / [U] (not-yet-evidenced) tags in prose, plus a short 'Status & evidence' section at the end of the root README for the load-bearing claims.
entries.rationale: Inline tags keep each claim honest at the point of reading; the section collects the load-bearing ones for scrutiny.

entries.decision: A markdown table (project | adopter | link | since) seeded with lume itself as the sole row, plus a one-line 'add yourself via PR' process. No lume verb to read it yet - deferred to a later workstream.
entries.rationale: Table is human-readable on GitHub/Obsidian and trivially parseable later; the single honest row IS the evidence for the 'zero external users' claim.
